title: Tetanus Neonatorum — Newborn with Trismus and Spasms

patient_data.chief_complaint: 7-day-old newborn unable to suck, jaw stiffness, generalized spasms
symptoms: Inability to suck or feed since yesterday
Opisthotonus triggered by touch or noise
Umbilical stump appears infected — purulent discharge
patient_data.history: Home birth attended by traditional birth attendant. Umbilical cord cut with unsterile instrument. Mother's vaccination status unknown. Rural area with no prenatal care.

should_detect: Neonatal tetanus — WHO elimination target disease
Onset day 3-14 of life with inability to suck + rigidity = classic presentation
Unsterile cord care is the source of infection
Treatment: tetanus immunoglobulin (TIG), wound care, diazepam for spasms
ICU admission — risk of respiratory failure
Maternal tetanus vaccination would have prevented this
